sections.paragraph:
ἀνατολή, ῆς, ἡ (s. ἀνατέλλω; poetic form ἀντ-, some mss. Pre-Socratics, Hdt.; ins, pap, LXX, En, TestSol 9:7 P; TestAbr A 11 p. 88, 28 [Stone p. 24]; TestJob, Test12Patr, JosAs; ApcEsdr 5:12 p. 30, 22 Tdf.; ApcMos, Philo, Joseph.; Mel., HE 4, 26, 14 [Fgm. 8b 43 = Goodsp., Apol. p. 309]).

① upward movement of celestial bodies, rising, of stars (Aeschyl. et al.; PHib 27, 45 πρὸς τ. δύσεις καὶ ἀνατολὰς τ. ἄστρων; PTebt 276, 38; Neugebauer-Hoesen index; PGM 13, 1027; 1037; Philo, Spec. Leg. 3, 187) ἐν τῇ ἀνατολῇ at its rising, when it rose Mt 2:2, because of the sg. and the article in contrast to ἀπὸ ἀνατολῶν, vs. 1, prob. not a geograph. expr. like the latter, but rather astronomical (B-D-R §235, 5; cp. B-D-F); likew. vs. 9; GJs 21:1, 3 (cp. Petosiris, Fgm. 6, ln. 31 of the moon ἅμα τῇ ἀνατολῇ=simultaneously with its rising; 12, ln. 133 ἐν τῇ τοῦ ἄστρου ἀνατολῇ; FBoll, ZNW 18, 1918, 44f; a distinction is also made by PGM 36, 239 ἐξ ἀνατολῆς τ. χωρίου πλησίον ἀνατολῶν ἡλίου. Cp. EHodous, CBQ 6, ’44, 81f [‘near the horizon’], and L-S-J-M s.v. 2).

② the position of the rising sun, east, orient (Hdt. et al.; LXX).

ⓐ sg. ἀπὸ ἀ. ἡλίου (cp. Aeschyl., Pr. 707 ἐνθένδʼ ἡλίου πρὸς ἀντολάς) from the east Rv 7:2; 16:12 (Just., D. 28, 5 [Mal 1:11 ἀνατολῶν]); simply ἀπὸ ἀ. (SIG 1112, 25) 21:13; (opp. δύσις; cp. Appian, Mithrid. 68 §288 ἀπό τε δύσεως καὶ ἐξ ἀνατολῆς; OGI 199, 32; Jos., Bell. 6, 301) short ending of Mk; πρὸς τὴν ἀ. toward the east (Jos., Ant. 1, 37, C. Ap. 1, 77) Hv 1, 4, 1; 3 (cp. Mel., HE 4, 26, 14). Gener. of the orient (opp. δύσις) 1 Cl 5:6; IRo 2:2.

ⓑ pl. (Hdt. et al.; Diod S 5, 42, 3; Jos., C. Ap. 1, 65; B-D-F §141, 2; Rob. 408) 1 Cl 10:4 (Gen 13:14). ἀπὸ ἀνατολῶν from the east (pap, s. Preis.; Gk. Parchments fr. Avroman IIa, 8: JHS 35, 1915, p. 30 ἀπὸ τ. ἀνατολῶν; Num 23:7) μάγοι ἀπὸ ἀ. Mt 2:1. ἐξέρχεσθαι ἀπὸ ἀ. come from the east (of lightning) Mt 24:27. ἀπὸ ἀ. καὶ δυσμῶν (this contrast Apollon. Rhod. 1, 85; Epict. 3, 13, 9; Sb 385, 2; Mal 1:11; Zech 8:7; Is 59:19; Philo, In Flacc. 45) from east and west=fr. the whole world Mt 8:11. The four points of the compass (Ps 106:3) Lk 13:29 (cp. En 18:6f εἰς ἀ. … πρὸς ἀ. Mel., P. 47, 335 [B] κατὰ ἀνατολὰς ἐν Ἐδέμ to the east, in Eden [on Gen. 2:8]).

③ a change from darkness to light in the early morning, the dawn, fig., of the coming of the Messiah (cp. Damasc., Vi. Isidori 244 φέρειν τ. θείαν ἀνατολήν [s. ἀνατέλλω 2]; of Augustus: Kaibel 978, 4 ὸ̔ς (ς)ωτ[ὴ]ρ Ζεὺ[ς ἀ]ν[έ]τ[ειλε] μέγας; [s. ἀνατέλλω 2]; Mel. Fgm. 8b, 45 περὶ λουτροῦ 4, Perler p. 232 = Goodsp., Apol. p. 311: ἥλιος ἀνατολῆς) ἀ. ἐξ ὕψους the dawn from heaven Lk 1:78, interpr. by AJacoby, ZNW 20, 1921, 205ff as sprout or scion of God, and sim. by Billerb. II, 1924, 113 as Messiah of Yahweh.—FDölger, Sol Salutis2, 1925, 149ff.—B. 871. DDD s.v.‘Helel’ (הילל). DELG s.v. τέλλω. M-M. TW. Sv.

Socratics Socratics , letters attributed to disciples of the phil. Socrates, various dates—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

En En = I Enoch—List 2

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

ApcEsdr ApcEsdr = Apocalypse of Esdras—List 2

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

ApcMos ApcMos = Apocalypse of Moses—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Goodsp., Apol. Goodsp., Apol. = Die ältesten Apologeten—List 5

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PHib PHib = The Hibeh Papyri I–II—List 4

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

  1. sg. = singular

prob. prob. = probable, probably

expr. expr. = expression

B-D-R B-D-R = FBlass, ADebrunner, FRehkopf, Grammatik des neutestamentlichen Griechisch—List 6

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

likew. likew. = likewise

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

Petosiris Petosiris = astrolog. writings attributed to P. and N.: collection of fragments; before II b.c.—List 5

  1. ln. = line

ZNW ZNW = Zeitschrift für die ntl. Wissenschaft—List 6

CBQ CBQ = Catholic Biblical Quarterly—List 6

L-S-J-M L-S-J-M = (L-S-J) HLiddell/RScott, A Greek-English Lexicon; New Edition by HSJones/RMcKenzie—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

opp. opp. = opposed to, opposite

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Hv Hv = Visions

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

IRo IRo = Ignatius to the Romans—List 1

  1. pl. = plural

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

Preis. Preis. = FPreisigke, Wörterbuch der griech. Papyrusurkunden—Lists 4, 6

Gk. Gk. = Greek

  1. fr. = from

JHS JHS = Journal of Hellenic Studies—List 6

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

Vi. Vi. = Vita, Vitae

Kaibel Kaibel = Epigrammata Graeca ex Lapidibus Conlecta, ed. GK.—List 3

interpr. interpr. = interpreted, interpretation

sim. sim. = similarly

Billerb. Billerb. = HLStrack and PBillerbeck, Kommentar z. NT aus Talmud u. Midrasch—List 6

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DDD DDD = Dictionary of Deities and Demons in the Bible—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 74
